⏺ mount A-

  Continuing ₢A-AAm. Through L3d-cleanup (commits 20eef3b9 + 7ec14b3b
  on top of L1+L2+L3a-c). Working tree clean. Skim 7ec14b3b first —
  dropped the redundant BUJB_RESOLVED_* publish layer; bujb references
  BURP_*/BURN_*/BURC_* directly. Combined `readonly VAR=value` is the
  canonical kindle form.

  Remaining work, in order:

  1. Tinder relocation in bujb_jurisdiction.sh. The `BUJB_lower_snake`
     pure literals (command= mappings, workload keypath suffixes, WSL
     distribution name, sshd_config hardening directives) currently live
     in `zbujb_kindle` but BCG places tinder at module top level. Move
     them. Use `Tools/buk/bubc_constants.sh` as the local exemplar.
     Runtime-dependent `ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_*` stay in kindle.

  2. L3e — audit `tt/buw-hj0`, `buhj_cli.sh`, `buhj_jurisdiction.sh`
     against BUSJH0. Likely already wired; verify.

  3. L4 per docket. BUS0 drift sweep (grep before completion), sub-letter
     legend, colophon retirement, RBK Windows orchestrator rewire.

  4. L5 verification: `bash -n`; `tt/buw-st`; `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh`
     unchanged from baseline.

  Notch at each layer boundary. Ask before wrapping.

  BCG (`Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md` — read end-to-end
  before editing bash). Tripwires from this session: no traps; no
  `2>/dev/null`; no awk/grep/tr (parameter expansion + case); no `$()`
  on externals (temp files); no multi-`local` per line; per-iteration
  loop locals OUTSIDE the loop with plain `local` (not `-r`); combined
  `readonly VAR=value` is canonical (don't name vars twice).

  Multi-line heredocs feeding ssh stdin are prohibited by BCG line 1690
  but established in bujb (garrison + fenestrate); match local pattern,
  flag the tension at notch.

## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Implementation. Single pace, layered scope: Regime → Module → Tabtargets
  → Spec/Integration → Verification.
  
  ## Docket
  
  Land the design from heat paddock and BUS0 spec (post-AAp split:
  fenestrate + garrison sibling verbs sharing one implementation module).
  
  ### L1. Regime & Constants
  
  - Implement four-field BURP, drop `BURN_WORKLOADS`, add
    `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` per paddock.
  - `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` enrolls as xname (1–32), format-checked by buv.
    No collision list, no allowed-set enumeration — it is just the SSH
    login name used to reach a node as the workload operator.
  - `bub` / `bube` / `bubep_*` tree minted; BUBC tinder constants placed
    (`BUBC_platforms_{windows,linux,mac}` holding `bubep_*`); `BURN_PLATFORM`
    re-enrolled with `bubep_linux bubep_mac bubep_windows`.
  
  ### L2. Module & Helper
  
  - `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh` BCG-compliant module per paddock.
    Sub-letter `b` = bash; legend added to BUS0 mapping section per Quoin
    Sub-Letter Discipline.
  - `bujb_resolve_investiture` is sole load-and-cross-validate entrypoint,
    exposes `BUJB_RESOLVED_*` globals (set listed in paddock), single-call-
    per-process, readonly after resolution.
  - Helper enforces at resolve-time: `BURP_VICEROYALTY` registered in BURN;
    key files exist with mode `0600`; privkey loads via `ssh-keygen -y` dry-
    load (proves parseable + unencrypted; replaces vague "key-pair self-
    consistency" wording — there is no second artifact, only the privkey);
    WSL default distribution = `rbtww-main`.
  
  ### L3. Tabtargets & Ceremonies
  
  - 14 tabtargets per paddock catalog: 11 net-new + 3 modify-in-place
    (`buw-rn[lrv]` already exist, reshape for new BURN four-field shape;
    `buw-rp[lrv]` are net-new alongside `buw-jpF`, `buw-jpG[bcw]`,
    `buw-jwk`, `buw-jwc`, `buw-jws`, `buw-hj0`).
  - Operational tabtargets call `bujb_resolve_investiture` first, then
    assert per-verb platform invariant inline (2-line check; helper stays
    generic): fenestrate = `bubep_windows`; garrison-b ∈ {`bubep_linux`,
    `bubep_mac`}; garrison-c/w = `bubep_windows`.
  - Fenestrate ceremony per BUSJPF two-phase contract; bash orchestrator
    treats post-restart disconnect as expected phase boundary.
  - Garrison ceremony per BUSJG[BCW] (workload-only, 6 steps); first admin-
    SSH failure surfaces a clear pointer to fenestrate handbook (Windows)
    or operator-managed `ssh-copy-id` (Linux/Mac).
  
  ### L4. Spec & Integration
  
  - BUS0 / BUSJGB / BUSJGC / BUSJGW specify the three concrete `command=`
    directive strings (b/c/w) as locked spec content, sourced by the
    module rather than invented there. `command=` is a documented OpenSSH
    `authorized_keys(5)` directive; standard usage.
  - BUS0 drift sweep folded in: `:burp_node:` linked term at line 127 →
    `:burp_viceroyalty:` (and any sibling stale `BURP_NODE` / `BURP_USER` /
    `BURP_SSH_PUBKEY` / `BURP_KEY_FILE` / `BURP_COMMAND` references AAp's
    sweep missed). Verify with `grep -n` before completion.
  - Superseded colophons retired (audit `buw-np*`, `buw-nw*`, old
    `buw-HW*`); AAm prunes only those directly replaced by the new 14-
    target catalog. Broad sweep deferred to AAn.
  - RBK Windows orchestrator (`rbhw_windows.sh`, `rbw-HWd[cdw]`,
    `rbw-h0`, `rbw-hw`) rewired to fenestrate + garrison + handbook.
  
  ### L5. Verification
  
  - `bash -n` green on all edited shell files.
  - `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` green.
  - `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` unchanged from baseline.
  
  ### Out of scope
  
  PowerShell garrison + dispatch (deferred); adopt verb (deferred); user
  regime (deferred); Cygwin/WSL sshd hardening verb (deferred — fenestrate
  is Windows-OpenSSH only); shell-letter availability discovery on BURN
  side; end-to-end operator test on a real Windows node (lives in
  AAD/E/F); broad cleanup sweep (lives in AAn); operator data migration
  (no migration — pre-release, nothing working to preserve).

7ec14b3b  ₢A-AAm    L3d cleanup of implement-jurisdiction: drop the redundant BUJB_RESOLVED_* publish block in bujb_resolve_investiture and replace every BUJB_RESOLVED_X reference with the underlying regime var (BURP_VICEROYALTY, BURN_HOST, BURN_PLATFORM, BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER, BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE, BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE, BURC_WORKLOAD_USER). The original L2 design published BUJB_RESOLVED_* as readonly snapshots of regime values 'after cross-validation,' but the regime vars are already readonly via each regime's lock-after-enforce step (BCG regime archetype) — the snapshot guarantee was vacuous, the parallel namespace was pure indirection, and SCREAMING+readonly defined in a runtime function violated BCG line 308 (kindle constants must be defined exclusively in kindle). Net effect: 7 declarations deleted, ~55 reference sites updated 1:1 across bujb_jurisdiction.sh (garrison helpers + steps + fenestrate exec/phases + orchestrators) and bujb_cli.sh (resolve diagnostic + knock + command_file + interactive_session). bujb_resolve_investiture shrinks to its essential contract: sentinels, single-call guard, ssh-keygen -y dry-load on the two key files (the validation that regime enforce structurally cannot perform), set ZBUJB_RESOLVED=1. The I-have-been-validated semantics survive in the sentinel alone. bujb_resolve diagnostic command's buc_doc_brief and buc_bare field labels updated to display the regime var names directly so operators see the same vocabulary the code references. zbujb_garrison_assert_platform comment line updated. bash -n green on both files; tt/buw-st green (5 fixtures, 28 cases); smoke on buw-jpF, buw-jpGb, buw-jwk all route correctly through the launcher and die cleanly on missing investiture. No behavioral change — every call site reads the same value as before, just under its canonical regime name.

Both helpers route through `powershell -NoProfile -File -` so the script body streams over stdin under cmd.exe (the default Windows OpenSSH shell). The two phase orchestrators implement the BUSJPF contract: zbujb_fenestrate_phase1 has chunk A (idempotent admin pubkey install in administrators_authorized_keys via PowerShell Test-Path/Add-Content with -notcontains dedup, icacls /inheritance:r /grant SYSTEM:F /grant Administrators:F lockdown, idempotent merge of three sshd_config directives via PowerShell ordered-hashtable + line-replace-if-match-or-append loop, sshd -t penultimate atomic op, Get-Content -Raw last op so stdout is clean) plus chunk B (Restart-Service sshd, exit code ignored — bash treats the disconnect as the expected phase boundary). zbujb_fenestrate_phase2 reconnects key-only after a 3-second sleep and re-emits Get-Content for round-trip verify. zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives parses the captured raw bytes via load-then-iterate (two arrays: directives_roll from BUJB_sshd_hardening, remote_lines_roll from CR-stripped temp file contents), then for each directive walks the remote lines with a case-glob match `"${z_directive} "*` and parameter expansion to extract the second field — no awk, no grep, no tr. Public bujb_fenestrate orchestrates phase1 + phase2 after zbujb_sentinel + ZBUJB_RESOLVED guard + platform invariant. bujb_cli.sh adds bujb_fenestrate_command (the CLI-side wrapper, named separately to avoid shadowing the module function — operator-facing dispatch through buz registration). Six new ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_* temp file kindle constants under BURD_TEMP_DIR (phase1 stdout/stderr, restart stdout/stderr, phase2 stdout/stderr) so SSH output capture stays out of $() and on disk for forensics. Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Implementation. Single pace, layered scope: Regime → Module → Tabtargets
  → Spec/Integration → Verification.
  
  ## Docket
  
  Land the design from heat paddock and BUS0 spec (post-AAp split:
  fenestrate + garrison sibling verbs sharing one implementation module).
  
  ### L1. Regime & Constants
  
  - Implement four-field BURP, drop `BURN_WORKLOADS`, add
    `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` per paddock.
  - `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` enrolls as xname (1–32), format-checked by buv.
    No collision list, no allowed-set enumeration — it is just the SSH
    login name used to reach a node as the workload operator.
  - `bub` / `bube` / `bubep_*` tree minted; BUBC tinder constants placed
    (`BUBC_platforms_{windows,linux,mac}` holding `bubep_*`); `BURN_PLATFORM`
    re-enrolled with `bubep_linux bubep_mac bubep_windows`.
  
  ### L2. Module & Helper
  
  - `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh` BCG-compliant module per paddock.
    Sub-letter `b` = bash; legend added to BUS0 mapping section per Quoin
    Sub-Letter Discipline.
  - `bujb_resolve_investiture` is sole load-and-cross-validate entrypoint,
    exposes `BUJB_RESOLVED_*` globals (set listed in paddock), single-call-
    per-process, readonly after resolution.
  - Helper enforces at resolve-time: `BURP_VICEROYALTY` registered in BURN;
    key files exist with mode `0600`; privkey loads via `ssh-keygen -y` dry-
    load (proves parseable + unencrypted; replaces vague "key-pair self-
    consistency" wording — there is no second artifact, only the privkey);
    WSL default distribution = `rbtww-main`.
  
  ### L3. Tabtargets & Ceremonies
  
  - 14 tabtargets per paddock catalog: 11 net-new + 3 modify-in-place
    (`buw-rn[lrv]` already exist, reshape for new BURN four-field shape;
    `buw-rp[lrv]` are net-new alongside `buw-jpF`, `buw-jpG[bcw]`,
    `buw-jwk`, `buw-jwc`, `buw-jws`, `buw-hj0`).
  - Operational tabtargets call `bujb_resolve_investiture` first, then
    assert per-verb platform invariant inline (2-line check; helper stays
    generic): fenestrate = `bubep_windows`; garrison-b ∈ {`bubep_linux`,
    `bubep_mac`}; garrison-c/w = `bubep_windows`.
  - Fenestrate ceremony per BUSJPF two-phase contract; bash orchestrator
    treats post-restart disconnect as expected phase boundary.
  - Garrison ceremony per BUSJG[BCW] (workload-only, 6 steps); first admin-
    SSH failure surfaces a clear pointer to fenestrate handbook (Windows)
    or operator-managed `ssh-copy-id` (Linux/Mac).
  
  ### L4. Spec & Integration
  
  - BUS0 / BUSJGB / BUSJGC / BUSJGW specify the three concrete `command=`
    directive strings (b/c/w) as locked spec content, sourced by the
    module rather than invented there. `command=` is a documented OpenSSH
    `authorized_keys(5)` directive; standard usage.
  - BUS0 drift sweep folded in: `:burp_node:` linked term at line 127 →
    `:burp_viceroyalty:` (and any sibling stale `BURP_NODE` / `BURP_USER` /
    `BURP_SSH_PUBKEY` / `BURP_KEY_FILE` / `BURP_COMMAND` references AAp's
    sweep missed). Verify with `grep -n` before completion.
  - Superseded colophons retired (audit `buw-np*`, `buw-nw*`, old
    `buw-HW*`); AAm prunes only those directly replaced by the new 14-
    target catalog. Broad sweep deferred to AAn.
  - RBK Windows orchestrator (`rbhw_windows.sh`, `rbw-HWd[cdw]`,
    `rbw-h0`, `rbw-hw`) rewired to fenestrate + garrison + handbook.
  
  ### L5. Verification
  
  - `bash -n` green on all edited shell files.
  - `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` green.
  - `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` unchanged from baseline.
  
  ### Out of scope
  
  PowerShell garrison + dispatch (deferred); adopt verb (deferred); user
  regime (deferred); Cygwin/WSL sshd hardening verb (deferred — fenestrate
  is Windows-OpenSSH only); shell-letter availability discovery on BURN
  side; end-to-end operator test on a real Windows node (lives in
  AAD/E/F); broad cleanup sweep (lives in AAn); operator data migration
  (no migration — pre-release, nothing working to preserve).

content.content:
# jjezs_paddock ₣A-

## Goal

Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and PowerShell access paths.

## Current Concept

**Jurisdiction** is the BUK feature-area for reaching out to remote nodes
deterministically. Logical-only umbrella concept; `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` is
its implementation seat (BCG-compliant module housing both verbs). Neither
the umbrella nor the implementation module earns AXLA-entity voicing in
BUS0. Architectural spine in BUS0 §Remote Node Access.

**Two regimes (BURN, BURP):**

- BURN — node-shape (host, OS family). Per-node, git-transported, changes
  rarely. Identifier: viceroyalty.
- BURP — privileged + workload credentials. Per-station-user. Four fields:
  `BURP_VICEROYALTY` (BURN reference), `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER`,
  `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`, `BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE`. Pubkeys derived from
  privkeys via `ssh-keygen -y` at use-time; no separate pubkey paths or inline
  strings. Identifier: investiture.

**BURC addition:**

- `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` — project-wide convention name for the workload OS
  user that garrison provisions on every node. Single source of truth.

**Two verbs (Fenestrate, Garrison):**

- **Fenestrate** — Windows-OpenSSH-only `sshd_config` harden plus admin SSH
  key-trust establishment. Uniform across shell-letters (no variant).
  Two-phase ceremony forced by `Restart-Service sshd` killing its own
  session. Tabtarget `buw-jpF` (capital F = persistent config application).
- **Garrison** — workload account provisioning only. Per-shell-letter
  (b/c/w). Never reads or writes any `sshd_config`; never alters admin
  authorized_keys. First action is admin SSH via key auth; failure surfaces
  immediately.
- For Linux/Mac there is no fenestrate verb — admin trust is operator-manual
  (e.g., `ssh-copy-id`); garrison runs against an existing key-trusted admin
  foothold. For Cygwin/WSL sshd hardening on Windows nodes (in addition to
  Windows OpenSSH), hardening is operator-manual.

**Garrison-destructive model:**

- Every garrison wipes prior workload state regardless of which investiture
  or operator runs it. Node runtime state belongs to whoever last garrisoned.
- One workload user per node, ever. No conscript / discharge / withdraw /
  vanquish / inventory verbs; garrison subsumes them.
- Admin-side sshd configuration is fenestrate's scope on Windows;
  operator-manual elsewhere.

**Cross-cutting premises:**

- `burn_host_singularity` — host of a node lives only in BURN; BURP reaches
  it by reference, never duplicates.
- `bus_keys_operator_owned` — system never generates or modifies SSH key
  material; operator owns all key administration.

**Tabtarget colophons (14 total; b/c/w shell support, PowerShell deferred):**

- `buw-r[np][lrv]` — BURN/BURP regime config (List/Render/Validate) — 6
- `buw-jpF` — Fenestrate (Windows OpenSSH only; uniform) — 1
- `buw-jpG[bcw]` — Garrison per shell-letter (b=native bash for Linux/Mac;
  c=Cygwin; w=WSL) — 3
- `buw-jwk` — Knock (probe workload reachability) — 1
- `buw-jwc` — Run command file as workload (shell determined by garrison) — 1
- `buw-jws` — Interactive SSH as workload — 1
- `buw-hj0` — Handbook jurisdiction top — 1

**Operational seat (`bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh`):**

BCG-compliant module housing both fenestrate and garrison implementations,
hardcoding the three shell-letter→`command=` directive mappings (b/c/w)
used by garrison on the workload account, the workload privkey destination
path on remote per shell-letter, the canonical WSL distribution name
(`rbtww-main`), and the Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config` hardening directive
set used by fenestrate. SSH transport mechanics live in code; BURP carries
no shell selection.

**Fenestrate ceremony (Windows OpenSSH only; uniform):**

Two-phase remote-run because `Restart-Service sshd` terminates its own
ssh session.

Phase 1 (over the password-or-key admin SSH session):

1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
   `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE` authenticates by key. First-run: ssh prompts
   on /dev/tty; operator types the Windows admin password once. Fenestrate
   process never sees the password.
2. Idempotently place admin pubkey (derived from `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`
   via `ssh-keygen -y`) in `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare entry
   (no `command=`; fenestrate is uniform). Performed before any sshd_config
   change so key auth is available for phase 2's reconnect.
3. `icacls` lockdown on `administrators_authorized_keys`.
4. Write hardened directives to `sshd_config`: `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
   `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Idempotent.
5. Verify written state via PowerShell `Get-Content` returning raw bytes,
   then bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracting effective directive
   values. Keeps PowerShell out of parsing (its error semantics are
   unreliable).
6. `sshd -t` validates the config (penultimate atomic op of phase 1; a
   malformed config aborts fenestrate before it could brick running sshd).
7. `Restart-Service sshd` (final atomic op of phase 1; ssh session
   terminates here — bash orchestrator treats the disconnect as the
   expected phase boundary).

Phase 2 (key auth only):

8. Reconnect as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey` (no password fallback). Failure
   here surfaces a brick — the operator's manual password no longer opens
   a session.
9. Verify post-state: the admin session authenticates by key alone and the
   running sshd serves the hardened configuration.

**Garrison ceremony (per shell-letter; workload-only):**

1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`; key auth
   only (no password fallback). On Windows, presupposes fenestrate has run
   for this investiture. On Linux/Mac, admin trust is operator-managed
   (e.g., `ssh-copy-id` placed the admin pubkey).
2. Destroy existing workload user (if present) via `userdel -r` or platform
   equivalent — removes account + home directory; stray files outside home
   are workload's own concern, not garrison's.
3. Create fresh workload user named `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` (unprivileged: no
   sudo, no admin group, ssh-only access).
4. Place workload pubkey in workload's authorized_keys with the
   shell-letter's `command=` directive.
5. Copy workload privkey to remote workload account at the path hardcoded in
   `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` per shell-letter (so workload can authenticate
   outbound to GitHub).
6. Validate round-trip — SSH as workload + exec no-op + verify exit 0.

## Heat Sequence

**Spec foundation:**
- spec-bus0-jurisdiction — BUS0 rewrite reflecting current concept
- introduce-fenestrate-narrow-garrison — split admin trust + sshd harden
  out of garrison into a uniform fenestrate verb

**Implementation:**
- implement-jurisdiction — four-field BURP, `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER`,
  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` module, 14 tabtargets, fenestrate + garrison
  ceremonies, cleanup superseded colophons + handbook entries

**Investigation (independent, runs anytime):**
- AAe — localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation diagnosis

**Practice (Windows host, blocks on implementation):**
- AAD — WSL distro + Cygwin install
- AAE — JJK fundus inside WSL
- AAF — Docker dual-daemon

## Deferred (named for future return)

- **PowerShell garrison + dispatch** — `buw-jpGp` tabtarget + the `p`
  shell-letter mapping in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Current heat ships b/c/w
  only.
- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged trust.
  Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their own machine.
- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin scenarios.
- **Separate workload→GitHub keypair** — current shape reuses workload SSH
  keypair for outbound; tighten to per-purpose separation if security demands.
- **BURP override fields for non-conventional key layouts** — operators with
  centralized SSH key storage, non-`.pub`-suffix layouts.
- **Localhost garrison/dispatch variants** — deferred; remote nodes only.
- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't duplicate.
- **Cygwin / WSL sshd hardening verb** — fenestrate covers Windows OpenSSH
  only. If c/w garrisons ever require their own sshd to be hardened
  programmatically (rather than operator-managed), that is a future
  fenestrate sibling.

## Standing Notes

- **Fenestrate handles first-time admin trust establishment on Windows.**
  On a fresh node, ssh's `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` falls
  through to a /dev/tty password prompt; operator types the Windows admin
  password once. Fenestrate then installs the admin pubkey, sets the icacls
  ACL, hardens sshd_config (`PasswordAuthentication no`), restarts sshd,
  and reconnects to verify by key. Subsequent runs use key auth
  automatically. The operator's manual Windows scope reduces to: install
  OpenSSH server, enable the service, set `PasswordAuthentication yes`
  temporarily, allow the firewall port, ensure the admin user has a known
  password.
- **Linux/Mac admin trust is operator-managed.** No fenestrate verb;
  operator places admin pubkey via `ssh-copy-id` (or equivalent), and
  garrison's first admin SSH succeeds by key alone.
- **Two-phase fenestrate is structural, not optional.** `Restart-Service
  sshd` kills its own ssh session, so any post-restart verification must
  happen over a fresh session. Bash orchestrator treats the post-restart
  disconnect as the expected phase boundary, not a failure.
- **Encrypted (passphrase-protected) privkeys not supported.** `ssh-keygen -y`
  prompts interactively; fenestrate and garrison would hang. Operator
  policy: privkeys unencrypted; station-level security covers
  privkey-at-rest protection.
- **Workload pubkey must be pre-registered with GitHub out-of-band.** Operator
  pastes the pubkey into GitHub (deploy key per repo or personal SSH key)
  before garrison runs. Garrison cannot validate this; it is a precondition.
- **WSL distribution is hardcoded to `rbtww-main`** in
  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. If the current default WSL distribution differs,
  garrison-WSL fails fast with a clear error.
- **Some `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` values may contain spaces** (e.g., Windows
  admin user `b hyslop`); audit shell quoting at every variable expansion and
  path construction.
- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as `100.x.y.z` /
  tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency.
- Fundus capability registry: `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSFR-FundusRegistry.md`.
- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the
  Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call.
- `command=` in authorized_keys is OpenSSH-standard (used by gitolite, GitHub,
  rsync, borg); shrinks key blast radius rather than expanding it.

# jjezs_pace ₢A-AAm

## Character

Implementation. Single pace, layered scope: Regime → Module → Tabtargets
→ Spec/Integration → Verification.

## Docket

Land the design from heat paddock and BUS0 spec (post-AAp split:
fenestrate + garrison sibling verbs sharing one implementation module).

### L1. Regime & Constants

- Implement four-field BURP, drop `BURN_WORKLOADS`, add
  `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` per paddock.
- `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` enrolls as xname (1–32), format-checked by buv.
  No collision list, no allowed-set enumeration — it is just the SSH
  login name used to reach a node as the workload operator.
- `bub` / `bube` / `bubep_*` tree minted; BUBC tinder constants placed
  (`BUBC_platforms_{windows,linux,mac}` holding `bubep_*`); `BURN_PLATFORM`
  re-enrolled with `bubep_linux bubep_mac bubep_windows`.

### L2. Module & Helper

- `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh` BCG-compliant module per paddock.
  Sub-letter `b` = bash; legend added to BUS0 mapping section per Quoin
  Sub-Letter Discipline.
- `bujb_resolve_investiture` is sole load-and-cross-validate entrypoint,
  exposes `BUJB_RESOLVED_*` globals (set listed in paddock), single-call-
  per-process, readonly after resolution.
- Helper enforces at resolve-time: `BURP_VICEROYALTY` registered in BURN;
  key files exist with mode `0600`; privkey loads via `ssh-keygen -y` dry-
  load (proves parseable + unencrypted; replaces vague "key-pair self-
  consistency" wording — there is no second artifact, only the privkey);
  WSL default distribution = `rbtww-main`.

### L3. Tabtargets & Ceremonies

- 14 tabtargets per paddock catalog: 11 net-new + 3 modify-in-place
  (`buw-rn[lrv]` already exist, reshape for new BURN four-field shape;
  `buw-rp[lrv]` are net-new alongside `buw-jpF`, `buw-jpG[bcw]`,
  `buw-jwk`, `buw-jwc`, `buw-jws`, `buw-hj0`).
- Operational tabtargets call `bujb_resolve_investiture` first, then
  assert per-verb platform invariant inline (2-line check; helper stays
  generic): fenestrate = `bubep_windows`; garrison-b ∈ {`bubep_linux`,
  `bubep_mac`}; garrison-c/w = `bubep_windows`.
- Fenestrate ceremony per BUSJPF two-phase contract; bash orchestrator
  treats post-restart disconnect as expected phase boundary.
- Garrison ceremony per BUSJG[BCW] (workload-only, 6 steps); first admin-
  SSH failure surfaces a clear pointer to fenestrate handbook (Windows)
  or operator-managed `ssh-copy-id` (Linux/Mac).

### L4. Spec & Integration

- BUS0 / BUSJGB / BUSJGC / BUSJGW specify the three concrete `command=`
  directive strings (b/c/w) as locked spec content, sourced by the
  module rather than invented there. `command=` is a documented OpenSSH
  `authorized_keys(5)` directive; standard usage.
- BUS0 drift sweep folded in: `:burp_node:` linked term at line 127 →
  `:burp_viceroyalty:` (and any sibling stale `BURP_NODE` / `BURP_USER` /
  `BURP_SSH_PUBKEY` / `BURP_KEY_FILE` / `BURP_COMMAND` references AAp's
  sweep missed). Verify with `grep -n` before completion.
- Superseded colophons retired (audit `buw-np*`, `buw-nw*`, old
  `buw-HW*`); AAm prunes only those directly replaced by the new 14-
  target catalog. Broad sweep deferred to AAn.
- RBK Windows orchestrator (`rbhw_windows.sh`, `rbw-HWd[cdw]`,
  `rbw-h0`, `rbw-hw`) rewired to fenestrate + garrison + handbook.

### L5. Verification

- `bash -n` green on all edited shell files.
- `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` green.
- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` unchanged from baseline.

### Out of scope

PowerShell garrison + dispatch (deferred); adopt verb (deferred); user
regime (deferred); Cygwin/WSL sshd hardening verb (deferred — fenestrate
is Windows-OpenSSH only); shell-letter availability discovery on BURN
side; end-to-end operator test on a real Windows node (lives in
AAD/E/F); broad cleanup sweep (lives in AAn); operator data migration
(no migration — pre-release, nothing working to preserve).

   values. Keeps PowerShell out of parsing (its error semantics are
   unreliable).
6. `sshd -t` validates the config (penultimate atomic op of phase 1; a
   malformed config aborts fenestrate before it could brick running sshd).
7. `Restart-Service sshd` (final atomic op of phase 1; ssh session
   terminates here — bash orchestrator treats the disconnect as the
   expected phase boundary).

Phase 2 (key auth only):

8. Reconnect as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey` (no password fallback). Failure
   here surfaces a brick — the operator's manual password no longer opens
   a session.
9. Verify post-state: the admin session authenticates by key alone and the
   running sshd serves the hardened configuration.

**Garrison ceremony (per shell-letter; workload-only):**

1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`; key auth
   only (no password fallback). On Windows, presupposes fenestrate has run
   for this investiture. On Linux/Mac, admin trust is operator-managed
   (e.g., `ssh-copy-id` placed the admin pubkey).
2. Destroy existing workload user (if present) via `userdel -r` or platform
   equivalent — removes account + home directory; stray files outside home
   are workload's own concern, not garrison's.
3. Create fresh workload user named `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` (unprivileged: no
   sudo, no admin group, ssh-only access).
4. Place workload pubkey in workload's authorized_keys with the
   shell-letter's `command=` directive.
5. Copy workload privkey to remote workload account at the path hardcoded in
   `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` per shell-letter (so workload can authenticate
   outbound to GitHub).
6. Validate round-trip — SSH as workload + exec no-op + verify exit 0.

## Heat Sequence

**Spec foundation:**
- spec-bus0-jurisdiction — BUS0 rewrite reflecting current concept
- introduce-fenestrate-narrow-garrison — split admin trust + sshd harden
  out of garrison into a uniform fenestrate verb

**Implementation:**
- implement-jurisdiction — four-field BURP, `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER`,
  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` module, 14 tabtargets, fenestrate + garrison
  ceremonies, cleanup superseded colophons + handbook entries

**Investigation (independent, runs anytime):**
- AAe — localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation diagnosis

**Practice (Windows host, blocks on implementation):**
- AAD — WSL distro + Cygwin install
- AAE — JJK fundus inside WSL
- AAF — Docker dual-daemon

## Deferred (named for future return)

- **PowerShell garrison + dispatch** — `buw-jpGp` tabtarget + the `p`
  shell-letter mapping in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Current heat ships b/c/w
  only.
- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged trust.
  Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their own machine.
- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin scenarios.
- **Separate workload→GitHub keypair** — current shape reuses workload SSH
  keypair for outbound; tighten to per-purpose separation if security demands.
- **BURP override fields for non-conventional key layouts** — operators with
  centralized SSH key storage, non-`.pub`-suffix layouts.
- **Localhost garrison/dispatch variants** — deferred; remote nodes only.
- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't duplicate.
- **Cygwin / WSL sshd hardening verb** — fenestrate covers Windows OpenSSH
  only. If c/w garrisons ever require their own sshd to be hardened
  programmatically (rather than operator-managed), that is a future
  fenestrate sibling.

## Standing Notes

- **Fenestrate handles first-time admin trust establishment on Windows.**
  On a fresh node, ssh's `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` falls
  through to a /dev/tty password prompt; operator types the Windows admin
  password once. Fenestrate then installs the admin pubkey, sets the icacls
  ACL, hardens sshd_config (`PasswordAuthentication no`), restarts sshd,
  and reconnects to verify by key. Subsequent runs use key auth
  automatically. The operator's manual Windows scope reduces to: install
  OpenSSH server, enable the service, set `PasswordAuthentication yes`
  temporarily, allow the firewall port, ensure the admin user has a known
  password.
- **Linux/Mac admin trust is operator-managed.** No fenestrate verb;
  operator places admin pubkey via `ssh-copy-id` (or equivalent), and
  garrison's first admin SSH succeeds by key alone.
- **Two-phase fenestrate is structural, not optional.** `Restart-Service
  sshd` kills its own ssh session, so any post-restart verification must
  happen over a fresh session. Bash orchestrator treats the post-restart
  disconnect as the expected phase boundary, not a failure.
- **Encrypted (passphrase-protected) privkeys not supported.** `ssh-keygen -y`
  prompts interactively; fenestrate and garrison would hang. Operator
  policy: privkeys unencrypted; station-level security covers
  privkey-at-rest protection.
- **Workload pubkey must be pre-registered with GitHub out-of-band.** Operator
  pastes the pubkey into GitHub (deploy key per repo or personal SSH key)
  before garrison runs. Garrison cannot validate this; it is a precondition.
- **WSL distribution is hardcoded to `rbtww-main`** in
  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. If the current default WSL distribution differs,
  garrison-WSL fails fast with a clear error.
- **Some `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` values may contain spaces** (e.g., Windows
  admin user `b hyslop`); audit shell quoting at every variable expansion and
  path construction.
- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as `100.x.y.z` /
  tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency.
- Fundus capability registry: `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSFR-FundusRegistry.md`.
- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the
  Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call.
- `command=` in authorized_keys is OpenSSH-standard (used by gitolite, GitHub,
  rsync, borg); shrinks key blast radius rather than expanding it.

# jjezs_pace ₢A-AAm

## Character

Implementation. Single pace, layered scope: Regime → Module → Tabtargets
→ Spec/Integration → Verification.

## Docket

Land the design from heat paddock and BUS0 spec (post-AAp split:
fenestrate + garrison sibling verbs sharing one implementation module).

### L1. Regime & Constants

- Implement four-field BURP, drop `BURN_WORKLOADS`, add
  `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` per paddock.
- `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` enrolls as xname (1–32), format-checked by buv.
  No collision list, no allowed-set enumeration — it is just the SSH
  login name used to reach a node as the workload operator.
- `bub` / `bube` / `bubep_*` tree minted; BUBC tinder constants placed
  (`BUBC_platforms_{windows,linux,mac}` holding `bubep_*`); `BURN_PLATFORM`
  re-enrolled with `bubep_linux bubep_mac bubep_windows`.

### L2. Module & Helper

- `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh` BCG-compliant module per paddock.
  Sub-letter `b` = bash; legend added to BUS0 mapping section per Quoin
  Sub-Letter Discipline.
- `bujb_resolve_investiture` is sole load-and-cross-validate entrypoint,
  exposes `BUJB_RESOLVED_*` globals (set listed in paddock), single-call-
  per-process, readonly after resolution.
- Helper enforces at resolve-time: `BURP_VICEROYALTY` registered in BURN;
  key files exist with mode `0600`; privkey loads via `ssh-keygen -y` dry-
  load (proves parseable + unencrypted; replaces vague "key-pair self-
  consistency" wording — there is no second artifact, only the privkey);
  WSL default distribution = `rbtww-main`.

### L3. Tabtargets & Ceremonies

- 14 tabtargets per paddock catalog: 11 net-new + 3 modify-in-place
  (`buw-rn[lrv]` already exist, reshape for new BURN four-field shape;
  `buw-rp[lrv]` are net-new alongside `buw-jpF`, `buw-jpG[bcw]`,
  `buw-jwk`, `buw-jwc`, `buw-jws`, `buw-hj0`).
- Operational tabtargets call `bujb_resolve_investiture` first, then
  assert per-verb platform invariant inline (2-line check; helper stays
  generic): fenestrate = `bubep_windows`; garrison-b ∈ {`bubep_linux`,
  `bubep_mac`}; garrison-c/w = `bubep_windows`.
- Fenestrate ceremony per BUSJPF two-phase contract; bash orchestrator
  treats post-restart disconnect as expected phase boundary.
- Garrison ceremony per BUSJG[BCW] (workload-only, 6 steps); first admin-
  SSH failure surfaces a clear pointer to fenestrate handbook (Windows)
  or operator-managed `ssh-copy-id` (Linux/Mac).

### L4. Spec & Integration

- BUS0 / BUSJGB / BUSJGC / BUSJGW specify the three concrete `command=`
  directive strings (b/c/w) as locked spec content, sourced by the
  module rather than invented there. `command=` is a documented OpenSSH
  `authorized_keys(5)` directive; standard usage.
- BUS0 drift sweep folded in: `:burp_node:` linked term at line 127 →
  `:burp_viceroyalty:` (and any sibling stale `BURP_NODE` / `BURP_USER` /
  `BURP_SSH_PUBKEY` / `BURP_KEY_FILE` / `BURP_COMMAND` references AAp's
  sweep missed). Verify with `grep -n` before completion.
- Superseded colophons retired (audit `buw-np*`, `buw-nw*`, old
  `buw-HW*`); AAm prunes only those directly replaced by the new 14-
  target catalog. Broad sweep deferred to AAn.
- RBK Windows orchestrator (`rbhw_windows.sh`, `rbw-HWd[cdw]`,
  `rbw-h0`, `rbw-hw`) rewired to fenestrate + garrison + handbook.

### L5. Verification

- `bash -n` green on all edited shell files.
- `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` green.
- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` unchanged from baseline.

### Out of scope

PowerShell garrison + dispatch (deferred); adopt verb (deferred); user
regime (deferred); Cygwin/WSL sshd hardening verb (deferred — fenestrate
is Windows-OpenSSH only); shell-letter availability discovery on BURN
side; end-to-end operator test on a real Windows node (lives in
AAD/E/F); broad cleanup sweep (lives in AAn); operator data migration
(no migration — pre-release, nothing working to preserve).

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#

#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUBC — BUK Bootstrap Constants
# Source-time literal constants for BUK kit layout.
# No kindle dependency — available immediately upon sourcing.

# Guard against multiple inclusion
test -z "${ZBUBC_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZBUBC_SOURCED=1

# Source-time literal constants
BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir="rbmn_nodes"
BUBC_rbmu_users_subdir="rbmu_users"

# Platform identifiers (bubep_* — bub/bube/bubep_* tree).
# These values are the canonical OS-family identifiers used in BURN_PLATFORM
# enrollment and in per-tabtarget platform invariant assertions.
# BUBC_platforms_<family> tinder constants provide single source of truth so
# code refers to the identifier by family name rather than hardcoding the
# literal token at every comparison site.
BUBC_platforms_linux="bubep_linux"
BUBC_platforms_mac="bubep_mac"
BUBC_platforms_windows="bubep_windows"

# Windows OpenSSH layout — forward slashes throughout so identical strings
# work in PowerShell, terminal display, and icacls invocations.
BUBC_windows_sshd_config='C:/ProgramData/ssh/sshd_config'
BUBC_windows_admin_auth_keys='C:/ProgramData/ssh/administrators_authorized_keys'
BUBC_windows_ssh_port="22"
BUBC_windows_fw_rule_name="sshd"
BUBC_windows_fw_display_name="OpenSSH Server"

# eof

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BUJB Jurisdiction Module - Implementation seat for fenestrate + garrison
20	#
21	# BCG-compliant module housing both BUS0 jurisdiction verbs (fenestrate,
22	# garrison) and the contracts not expressed in regime data:
23	# the three shell-letter -> command= directive mappings (b/c/w),
24	# the workload privkey destination paths on the remote per shell-letter,
25	# the canonical WSL distribution name, and the Windows OpenSSH sshd_config
26	# hardening directive set.
27	#
28	# Sub-letter b in bujb signals the bash-format implementation. A future
29	# PowerShell sibling would mint as bujp_jurisdiction.ps1.
30	
31	set -euo pipefail
32	
33	# Multiple inclusion detection
34	test -z "${ZBUJB_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module bujb multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
35	ZBUJB_SOURCED=1
36	
37	######################################################################
38	# Internal Functions (zbujb_*)
39	
40	zbujb_kindle() {
41	  test -z "${ZBUJB_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module bujb already kindled"
42	
43	  # Shell-letter -> command= directive mappings.
44	  # Forced commands routed through SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND keep workload account
45	  # behaviour pinned to the chosen shell regardless of what the SSH client
46	  # requests. Locked spec content; mirrored in BUSJG{B,C,W}.
47	  readonly BUJB_command_b='command="/bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding'
48	  readonly BUJB_command_c='command="C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -c \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding'
49	  readonly BUJB_command_w='command="C:/Windows/System32/wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --exec /bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding'
50	
51	  # Shell-letter -> workload privkey destination path on the remote
52	  # (relative to the workload account home directory).
53	  readonly BUJB_workload_keypath_b='.ssh/id_ed25519'
54	  readonly BUJB_workload_keypath_c='.ssh/id_ed25519'
55	  readonly BUJB_workload_keypath_w='.ssh/id_ed25519'
56	
57	  # Canonical WSL distribution name reached by garrison-w.
58	  readonly BUJB_wsl_distribution='rbtww-main'
59	
60	  # Windows OpenSSH sshd_config hardening directive set written by
61	  # fenestrate phase 1. Newline-joined; each directive is asserted by
62	  # bash-side parse after PowerShell Get-Content returns the raw bytes.
63	  readonly BUJB_sshd_hardening='PubkeyAuthentication yes
64	PasswordAuthentication no
65	PermitEmptyPasswords no'
66	
67	  # Fenestrate temp file paths — ssh stdout/stderr captured here so
68	  # callers parse from disk (no `$(ssh ...)` capture).
69	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase1_stdout.txt"
70	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase1_stderr.txt"
71	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_restart_stdout.txt"
72	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_restart_stderr.txt"
73	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase2_stdout.txt"
74	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase2_stderr.txt"
75	
76	  readonly ZBUJB_KINDLED=1
77	}
78	
79	zbujb_sentinel() {
80	  test "${ZBUJB_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module bujb not kindled - call zbujb_kindle first"
81	}
82	
83	# zbujb_check_key_file PATH VARNAME -- validate SSH private key at PATH:
84	# exists, mode 0600, parseable + unencrypted (ssh-keygen -y dry-load with
85	# empty passphrase). Diagnostic uses VARNAME as the field label.
86	zbujb_check_key_file() {
87	  local z_path="${1:-}"
88	  local z_varname="${2:-}"
89	
90	  test -n "${z_path}"   || buc_die "${z_varname}: empty path"
91	  test -f "${z_path}"   || buc_die "${z_varname}: file not found: ${z_path}"
92	
93	  local z_mode=""
94	  if z_mode=$(stat -f '%A' "${z_path}" 2>/dev/null); then
95	    : # macOS / BSD stat
96	  elif z_mode=$(stat -c '%a' "${z_path}" 2>/dev/null); then
97	    : # GNU/Linux stat
98	  else
99	    buc_die "${z_varname}: cannot stat ${z_path}"
100	  fi
101	  test "${z_mode}" = "600" || buc_die "${z_varname}: mode must be 0600, got ${z_mode}: ${z_path}"
102	
103	  ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${z_path}" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
104	    || buc_die "${z_varname}: ssh-keygen -y dry-load failed (passphrase-protected or malformed): ${z_path}"
105	}
106	
107	######################################################################
108	# Public Functions (bujb_*)
109	
110	# bujb_resolve_investiture -- sole runtime cross-validation entrypoint.
111	#
112	# Preconditions: BURC, BURN, BURP regimes already kindled and enforced
113	# (the bujb_cli furnish handles this for tabtarget callers; the cli's
114	# BURP and BURN sourcing also implicitly cross-validates that
115	# BURP_VICEROYALTY refers to a registered BURN profile by file presence).
116	#
117	# Behaviour: validates BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE and BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE
118	# (exist, mode 0600, ssh-keygen -y dry-load proves parseable + unencrypted) —
119	# the validation regime enforce cannot do because it requires running
120	# ssh-keygen against the private key. After validation, sets ZBUJB_RESOLVED=1
121	# as the I-have-been-validated sentinel. Callers reference the regime vars
122	# directly (BURP_*, BURN_*, BURC_*); no separate published namespace.
123	# Single-call-per-process; subsequent calls die.
124	bujb_resolve_investiture() {
125	  zbujb_sentinel
126	  zburc_sentinel
127	  zburn_sentinel
128	  zburp_sentinel
129	
130	  test -z "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" \
131	    || buc_die "bujb_resolve_investiture already called - single-call-per-process"
132	
133	  zbujb_check_key_file "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}" "BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE"
134	  zbujb_check_key_file "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"   "BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE"
135	
136	  readonly ZBUJB_RESOLVED=1
137	}
138	
139	# bujb_command_for SHELL_LETTER -- emit the workload authorized_keys
140	# command= directive for shell-letter b, c, or w (echoed to stdout).
141	bujb_command_for() {
142	  zbujb_sentinel
143	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
144	  case "${z_letter}" in
145	    b) echo "${BUJB_command_b}" ;;
146	    c) echo "${BUJB_command_c}" ;;
147	    w) echo "${BUJB_command_w}" ;;
148	    *) buc_die "bujb_command_for: invalid shell-letter (expected b/c/w): '${z_letter}'" ;;
149	  esac
150	}
151	
152	# bujb_workload_keypath_for SHELL_LETTER -- emit the workload privkey
153	# remote destination path (relative to workload home) for b, c, or w.
154	bujb_workload_keypath_for() {
155	  zbujb_sentinel
156	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
157	  case "${z_letter}" in
158	    b) echo "${BUJB_workload_keypath_b}" ;;
159	    c) echo "${BUJB_workload_keypath_c}" ;;
160	    w) echo "${BUJB_workload_keypath_w}" ;;
161	    *) buc_die "bujb_workload_keypath_for: invalid shell-letter (expected b/c/w): '${z_letter}'" ;;
162	  esac
163	}
164	
165	######################################################################
166	# Internal: Garrison helpers
167	
168	zbujb_assert_shell_letter() {
169	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
170	  case "${z_letter}" in
171	    b|c|w) ;;
172	    *) buc_die "Invalid shell-letter (expected b/c/w): '${z_letter}'" ;;
173	  esac
174	}
175	
176	# zbujb_garrison_assert_platform LETTER -- assert BURN_PLATFORM
177	# matches the shell-letter's required platform set.
178	zbujb_garrison_assert_platform() {
179	  zbujb_sentinel
180	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
181	  zbujb_assert_shell_letter "${z_letter}"
182	  case "${z_letter}" in
183	    b)
184	      case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
185	        bubep_linux|bubep_mac) ;;
186	        *) buc_die "garrison-b requires bubep_linux or bubep_mac, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'" ;;
187	      esac
188	      ;;
189	    c|w)
190	      test "${BURN_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
191	        || buc_die "garrison-${z_letter} requires bubep_windows, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
192	      ;;
193	  esac
194	}
195	
196	# zbujb_workload_home LETTER -- echo the absolute workload home path on the
197	# remote node for the given shell-letter.
198	zbujb_workload_home() {
199	  zbujb_sentinel
200	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
201	  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
202	  case "${z_letter}" in
203	    b)
204	      case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
205	        bubep_linux) echo "/home/${z_wlu}" ;;
206	        bubep_mac)   echo "/Users/${z_wlu}" ;;
207	        *)           buc_die "zbujb_workload_home: unsupported platform '${BURN_PLATFORM}'" ;;
208	      esac
209	      ;;
210	    c|w) echo "/home/${z_wlu}" ;;
211	    *)   buc_die "zbujb_workload_home: invalid letter '${z_letter}'" ;;
212	  esac
213	}
214	
215	# zbujb_admin_exec LETTER -- read a bash script from stdin and execute it on
216	# the remote node as the privileged user, wrapped in the shell-letter-
217	# appropriate invocation (native bash for b; Cygwin's bash --login for c;
218	# wsl.exe to BUJB_wsl_distribution as root for w).
219	zbujb_admin_exec() {
220	  zbujb_sentinel
221	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
222	  zbujb_assert_shell_letter "${z_letter}"
223	
224	  local z_remote_invoker
225	  case "${z_letter}" in
226	    b) z_remote_invoker='bash -s' ;;
227	    c) z_remote_invoker='C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -s' ;;
228	    w) z_remote_invoker="wsl.exe --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user root bash -s" ;;
229	  esac
230	
231	  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"     \
232	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                         \
233	      -o BatchMode=yes                              \
234	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new           \
235	      -o ConnectTimeout=15                          \
236	      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
237	      "${z_remote_invoker}"
238	}
239	
240	######################################################################
241	# Internal: Garrison steps (6-step ceremony per BUSJG{B,C,W})
242	
243	# Step 1 -- open admin SSH (test reachability under key-only auth).
244	zbujb_garrison_step1_admin_open() {
245	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
246	  buc_step "  [1/6] Open admin SSH (${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST})"
247	
248	  local z_exit=0
249	  zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<<'exit 0' || z_exit=$?
250	  if test "${z_exit}" -ne 0; then
251	    case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
252	      bubep_windows)
253	        buc_die "Admin SSH failed (exit ${z_exit}). Run fenestrate first: tt/buw-jpF.Fenestrate.sh ${BURP_VICEROYALTY}"
254	        ;;
255	      *)
256	        buc_die "Admin SSH failed (exit ${z_exit}). Place admin pubkey via 'ssh-copy-id -i ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}.pub ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}' first."
257	        ;;
258	    esac
259	  fi
260	}
261	
262	# Step 2 -- destroy any existing workload account + home.
263	zbujb_garrison_step2_destroy() {
264	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
265	  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
266	  local z_wlhome
267	  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
268	  buc_step "  [2/6] Destroy workload (${z_wlu})"
269	
270	  case "${z_letter}" in
271	    b)
272	      zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
273	set -uo pipefail
274	sudo -n userdel -r '${z_wlu}' 2>/dev/null || true
275	sudo -n rm -rf '${z_wlhome}' 2>/dev/null || true
276	SCRIPT
277	      ;;
278	    c)
279	      # Cygwin workload is a Windows local user; delete via net.exe then
280	      # purge the Cygwin home directory.
281	      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
282	set -uo pipefail
283	net.exe user '${z_wlu}' /delete > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
284	rm -rf '${z_wlhome}'                              2>/dev/null || true
285	rm -rf '/cygdrive/c/cygwin64/home/${z_wlu}'       2>/dev/null || true
286	SCRIPT
287	      ;;
288	    w)
289	      zbujb_admin_exec w <<SCRIPT
290	set -uo pipefail
291	userdel -r '${z_wlu}' 2>/dev/null || true
292	rm -rf '${z_wlhome}'  2>/dev/null || true
293	SCRIPT
294	      ;;
295	  esac
296	}
297	
298	# Step 3 -- create the workload account fresh, ssh-only, no privilege.
299	zbujb_garrison_step3_create() {
300	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
301	  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
302	  buc_step "  [3/6] Create workload (${z_wlu})"
303	
304	  case "${z_letter}" in
305	    b)
306	      case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
307	        bubep_linux)
308	          zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
309	set -euo pipefail
310	sudo -n useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash '${z_wlu}'
311	sudo -n passwd  --lock '${z_wlu}'
312	SCRIPT
313	          ;;
314	        bubep_mac)
315	          # Mac uses dscl/sysadminctl; left for in-environment refinement.
316	          # Operator may need to seat a more idiomatic primary group ID.
317	          zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
318	set -euo pipefail
319	sudo -n sysadminctl -addUser '${z_wlu}' -roleAccount
320	sudo -n dscl . -create '/Users/${z_wlu}' UserShell /bin/bash
321	SCRIPT
322	          ;;
323	      esac
324	      ;;
325	    c)
326	      # Cygwin reflects Windows user accounts; mint via net.exe with a
327	      # disabled-password posture (we want ssh-key-only).
328	      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
329	set -euo pipefail
330	net.exe user '${z_wlu}' /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes > /dev/null
331	mkpasswd -l -u '${z_wlu}' >> /etc/passwd
332	mkdir -p '/home/${z_wlu}'
333	chown -R '${z_wlu}' '/home/${z_wlu}'
334	SCRIPT
335	      ;;
336	    w)
337	      zbujb_admin_exec w <<SCRIPT
338	set -euo pipefail
339	useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash '${z_wlu}'
340	passwd  --lock '${z_wlu}'
341	SCRIPT
342	      ;;
343	  esac
344	}
345	
346	# Step 4 -- write workload authorized_keys with the shell-letter command=
347	# directive and the workload pubkey (derived locally from the privkey).
348	zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust() {
349	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
350	  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
351	  local z_wlhome
352	  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
353	  buc_step "  [4/6] Place workload trust (${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys)"
354	
355	  local z_command_directive
356	  z_command_directive=$(bujb_command_for "${z_letter}")
357	
358	  local z_pubkey
359	  z_pubkey=$(ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}") \
360	    || buc_die "ssh-keygen -y failed for workload key: ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"
361	
362	  local z_authkeys_line="${z_command_directive} ${z_pubkey}"
363	
364	  case "${z_letter}" in
365	    b|w)
366	      zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<SCRIPT
367	set -euo pipefail
368	$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")mkdir -p   '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
369	$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")chmod 700  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
370	echo '${z_authkeys_line}' | $([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")tee '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys' > /dev/null
371	$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")chmod 600  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
372	$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")chown -R '${z_wlu}:${z_wlu}' '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
373	SCRIPT
374	      ;;
375	    c)
376	      # In Cygwin, file ownership/permissions interplay with NTFS ACLs.
377	      # mkdir/chmod/chown are Cygwin-mediated; run as the admin user
378	      # without sudo (Windows admins already have privilege).
379	      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
380	set -euo pipefail
381	mkdir -p   '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
382	chmod 700  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
383	echo '${z_authkeys_line}' > '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
384	chmod 600  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
385	chown -R '${z_wlu}' '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
386	SCRIPT
387	      ;;
388	  esac
389	}
390	
391	# Step 5 -- copy workload privkey to the remote at the shell-letter's
392	# hardcoded destination path, with workload ownership and 0600 mode.
393	zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key() {
394	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
395	  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
396	  local z_wlhome
397	  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
398	  local z_keypath
399	  z_keypath=$(bujb_workload_keypath_for "${z_letter}")
400	  local z_target="${z_wlhome}/${z_keypath}"
401	  buc_step "  [5/6] Plant workload privkey (${z_target})"
402	
403	  local z_key_b64
404	  z_key_b64=$(base64 < "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}") \
405	    || buc_die "base64 encode failed for workload key: ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"
406	
407	  case "${z_letter}" in
408	    b|w)
409	      local z_sudo=""
410	      test "${z_letter}" = "b" && z_sudo="sudo -n"
411	      zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<SCRIPT
412	set -euo pipefail
413	ztmp=\$(mktemp)
414	trap 'rm -f "\${ztmp}"' EXIT
415	echo '${z_key_b64}' | base64 -d > "\${ztmp}"
416	${z_sudo} mkdir -p   '$(dirname "${z_target}")'
417	${z_sudo} install -m 600 -o '${z_wlu}' -g '${z_wlu}' "\${ztmp}" '${z_target}'
418	SCRIPT
419	      ;;
420	    c)
421	      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
422	set -euo pipefail
423	ztmp=\$(mktemp)
424	trap 'rm -f "\${ztmp}"' EXIT
425	echo '${z_key_b64}' | base64 -d > "\${ztmp}"
426	mkdir -p   '$(dirname "${z_target}")'
427	cp "\${ztmp}" '${z_target}'
428	chmod 600  '${z_target}'
429	chown      '${z_wlu}' '${z_target}'
430	SCRIPT
431	      ;;
432	  esac
433	}
434	
435	# Step 6 -- workload-side round-trip validation (knock).
436	zbujb_garrison_step6_validate() {
437	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
438	  buc_step "  [6/6] Validate workload round-trip"
439	
440	  local z_exit=0
441	  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
442	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
443	      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
444	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
445	      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
446	      "${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
447	      true                                        \
448	    || z_exit=$?
449	
450	  test "${z_exit}" -eq 0 \
451	    || buc_die "Workload round-trip failed (ssh exit ${z_exit}); the new account did not accept its own key."
452	}
453	
454	######################################################################
455	# Public: Garrison ceremony
456	
457	# bujb_garrison LETTER -- run the 6-step garrison ceremony for shell-letter
458	# b, c, or w. Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
459	bujb_garrison() {
460	  zbujb_sentinel
461	  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
462	    || buc_die "bujb_garrison: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
463	
464	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
465	  zbujb_garrison_assert_platform "${z_letter}"
466	
467	  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter}: ${BURP_VICEROYALTY} (${BURN_HOST})"
468	
469	  zbujb_garrison_step1_admin_open    "${z_letter}"
470	  zbujb_garrison_step2_destroy       "${z_letter}"
471	  zbujb_garrison_step3_create        "${z_letter}"
472	  zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust   "${z_letter}"
473	  zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key     "${z_letter}"
474	  zbujb_garrison_step6_validate      "${z_letter}"
475	
476	  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter} succeeded"
477	}
478	
479	######################################################################
480	# Internal: Fenestrate helpers (BUSJPF — Windows OpenSSH only)
481	#
482	# Each phase 1 chunk is a separate ssh call with publickey,password preferred
483	# auth. On a fresh node, chunk A's first publickey attempt fails and ssh
484	# falls through to /dev/tty password prompt — operator types once. After
485	# chunk A places the admin pubkey, chunk B's publickey attempt succeeds
486	# (the running sshd's old config still permits pubkey auth and now reads
487	# the updated authorized_keys), so no further prompt. Phase 2 is key-only.
488	# No ControlMaster, no traps, no 2>/dev/null.
489	
490	zbujb_fenestrate_assert_platform() {
491	  zbujb_sentinel
492	  test "${BURN_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
493	    || buc_die "fenestrate requires bubep_windows, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
494	}
495	
496	# zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback STDOUT_FILE STDERR_FILE
497	# Reads a PowerShell script from stdin and runs it on the remote node as
498	# the privileged user. publickey,password preferred (BatchMode=no allows
499	# /dev/tty password prompt on first run). Default Windows OpenSSH shell is
500	# cmd.exe; we explicitly invoke `powershell -NoProfile -File -` to feed
501	# the script via stdin. Returns ssh's exit code (caller decides).
502	zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback() {
503	  zbujb_sentinel
504	  local -r z_stdout_file="${1:-}"
505	  local -r z_stderr_file="${2:-}"
506	  test -n "${z_stdout_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback: stdout_file required"
507	  test -n "${z_stderr_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback: stderr_file required"
508	
509	  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"            \
510	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                                \
511	      -o BatchMode=no                                      \
512	      -o PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password       \
513	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new                  \
514	      -o ConnectTimeout=15                                 \
515	      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
516	      'powershell -NoProfile -File -'                      \
517	      > "${z_stdout_file}"                                 \
518	      2> "${z_stderr_file}"
519	}
520	
521	# zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly STDOUT_FILE STDERR_FILE
522	# Same as above but BatchMode=yes (no password fallback). Used for phase 2.
523	zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly() {
524	  zbujb_sentinel
525	  local -r z_stdout_file="${1:-}"
526	  local -r z_stderr_file="${2:-}"
527	  test -n "${z_stdout_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly: stdout_file required"
528	  test -n "${z_stderr_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly: stderr_file required"
529	
530	  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"            \
531	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                                \
532	      -o BatchMode=yes                                     \
533	      -o PreferredAuthentications=publickey                \
534	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new                  \
535	      -o ConnectTimeout=15                                 \
536	      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
537	      'powershell -NoProfile -File -'                      \
538	      > "${z_stdout_file}"                                 \
539	      2> "${z_stderr_file}"
540	}
541	
542	# zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives REMOTE_FILE -- load REMOTE_FILE (raw
543	# sshd_config bytes from PowerShell Get-Content), strip CR, then assert
544	# each directive in BUJB_sshd_hardening appears with the expected value.
545	# Load-then-iterate (no nested while-read on stdin); pure parameter
546	# expansion + case (no awk/grep/tr).
547	zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives() {
548	  zbujb_sentinel
549	  local -r z_remote_file="${1:-}"
550	  test -n "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives: remote_file required"
551	  test -f "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives: remote_file not found: ${z_remote_file}"
552	
553	  local -r z_raw_bytes=$(<"${z_remote_file}")
554	  test -n "${z_raw_bytes}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives: empty remote sshd_config bytes: ${z_remote_file}"
555	  local -r z_clean_bytes="${z_raw_bytes//$'\r'/}"
556	
557	  local z_directives_roll=()
558	  local z_pair=""
559	  while IFS= read -r z_pair || test -n "${z_pair}"; do
560	    test -n "${z_pair}" || continue
561	    z_directives_roll+=("${z_pair}")
562	  done <<<"${BUJB_sshd_hardening}"
563	
564	  local z_remote_lines_roll=()
565	  local z_line=""
566	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
567	    z_remote_lines_roll+=("${z_line}")
568	  done <<<"${z_clean_bytes}"
569	
570	  local z_directive=""
571	  local z_expected=""
572	  local z_actual=""
573	  local z_after_directive=""
574	  local z_i=0
575	  local z_j=0
576	  for z_i in "${!z_directives_roll[@]}"; do
577	    z_directive="${z_directives_roll[$z_i]%% *}"
578	    z_expected="${z_directives_roll[$z_i]#* }"
579	    z_actual=""
580	
581	    for z_j in "${!z_remote_lines_roll[@]}"; do
582	      case "${z_remote_lines_roll[$z_j]}" in
583	        "${z_directive} "*)
584	          z_after_directive="${z_remote_lines_roll[$z_j]#"${z_directive} "}"
585	          z_actual="${z_after_directive%% *}"
586	          break
587	          ;;
588	      esac
589	    done
590	
591	    test -n "${z_actual}" \
592	      || buc_die "Hardening verify: directive '${z_directive}' missing or commented in remote sshd_config"
593	    test "${z_actual}" = "${z_expected}" \
594	      || buc_die "Hardening verify: ${z_directive}: expected '${z_expected}', got '${z_actual}'"
595	  done
596	}
597	
598	######################################################################
599	# Internal: Fenestrate phases
600	
601	# zbujb_fenestrate_phase1 -- chunk A (install admin pubkey idempotently +
602	# icacls + merge sshd_config hardening + sshd -t + emit raw bytes); bash-
603	# side parse + verify; then chunk B (Restart-Service sshd, disconnect
604	# expected — exit code ignored).
605	zbujb_fenestrate_phase1() {
606	  zbujb_sentinel
607	
608	  local z_pubkey=""
609	  z_pubkey=$(ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}") \
610	    || buc_die "ssh-keygen -y failed for admin key: ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"
611	
612	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Chunk A: pubkey + icacls + sshd_config + sshd -t + emit (operator may be prompted for admin password on first run)"
613	
614	  zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback \
615	      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDOUT}"      \
616	      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDERR}"      \
617	    <<PS1 || buc_die "Phase 1 chunk A failed — admin pubkey/icacls/sshd_config/sshd -t did not all succeed; see ${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDERR}"
618	\$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
619	
620	\$pubkey = '${z_pubkey}'
621	\$adminAuthKeys = "\$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\administrators_authorized_keys"
622	\$sshConfig    = "\$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\sshd_config"
623	
624	# Idempotent admin pubkey install
625	if (-not (Test-Path \$adminAuthKeys)) {
626	  New-Item -Path \$adminAuthKeys -ItemType File -Force | Out-Null
627	}
628	\$existingLines = @(Get-Content \$adminAuthKeys -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
629	if (\$existingLines -notcontains \$pubkey) {
630	  Add-Content -Path \$adminAuthKeys -Value \$pubkey -Encoding ASCII
631	}
632	
633	# icacls lockdown (idempotent)
634	icacls \$adminAuthKeys /inheritance:r /grant 'SYSTEM:F' /grant 'BUILTIN\\Administrators:F' 2>&1 | Out-Null
635	if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "icacls failed (exit \$LASTEXITCODE)" }
636	
637	# Idempotent sshd_config harden — replace the first matching line for
638	# each directive (commented or otherwise) and drop subsequent duplicates;
639	# append directives that are absent. Convergent on re-run.
640	\$directives = [ordered]@{
641	  'PubkeyAuthentication'   = 'yes'
642	  'PasswordAuthentication' = 'no'
643	  'PermitEmptyPasswords'   = 'no'
644	}
645	
646	\$lines = @(Get-Content \$sshConfig)
647	\$out   = New-Object System.Collections.Generic.List[string]
648	\$seen  = @{}
649	foreach (\$line in \$lines) {
650	  \$matched = \$false
651	  foreach (\$k in \$directives.Keys) {
652	    if (\$line -match "^\\s*#*\\s*\$k\\s+\\S") {
653	      if (-not \$seen.ContainsKey(\$k)) {
654	        \$out.Add("\$k \$(\$directives[\$k])")
655	        \$seen[\$k] = \$true
656	      }
657	      \$matched = \$true
658	      break
659	    }
660	  }
661	  if (-not \$matched) { \$out.Add(\$line) }
662	}
663	foreach (\$k in \$directives.Keys) {
664	  if (-not \$seen.ContainsKey(\$k)) { \$out.Add("\$k \$(\$directives[\$k])") }
665	}
666	Set-Content -Path \$sshConfig -Value \$out -Encoding ASCII
667	
668	# sshd -t — penultimate atomic op; aborts before restart on bad config
669	& 'C:\\Windows\\System32\\OpenSSH\\sshd.exe' -t
670	if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "sshd -t failed (exit \$LASTEXITCODE)" }
671	
672	# Emit raw bytes for bash-side parse (last op so output is clean)
673	Get-Content \$sshConfig -Raw
674	PS1
675	
676	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Verify hardened directives via bash-side parse"
677	  zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDOUT}"
678	
679	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Chunk B: Restart-Service sshd (disconnect expected — exit code ignored)"
680	  zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback \
681	      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDOUT}"     \
682	      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDERR}"     \
683	    <<'PS1' || true
684	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'
685	Restart-Service sshd
686	PS1
687	}
688	
689	# zbujb_fenestrate_phase2 -- reconnect via key-only auth and re-verify the
690	# hardened directives served by the running sshd.
691	zbujb_fenestrate_phase2() {
692	  zbujb_sentinel
693	
694	  buc_step "  [Phase 2] Reconnect under key-only auth + re-verify"
695	
696	  # Allow sshd a moment to come back from Restart-Service.
697	  sleep 3
698	
699	  zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly                 \
700	      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDOUT}"       \
701	      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDERR}"       \
702	    <<'PS1' || buc_die "Phase 2 reconnect failed — possible brick: admin pubkey not honored after restart or sshd did not come back up; see ${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDERR}"
703	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
704	Get-Content "$env:ProgramData\ssh\sshd_config" -Raw
705	PS1
706	
707	  zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDOUT}"
708	}
709	
710	######################################################################
711	# Public: Fenestrate ceremony
712	
713	# bujb_fenestrate -- run the two-phase fenestrate ceremony for a Windows
714	# OpenSSH node. Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
715	bujb_fenestrate() {
716	  zbujb_sentinel
717	  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
718	    || buc_die "bujb_fenestrate: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
719	
720	  zbujb_fenestrate_assert_platform
721	
722	  buc_step "Fenestrate: ${BURP_VICEROYALTY} (${BURN_HOST})"
723	
724	  zbujb_fenestrate_phase1
725	  zbujb_fenestrate_phase2
726	
727	  buc_step "Fenestrate succeeded"
728	}
729	
730	# eof
731
